diet_advice.current_diet_assessment: Daily consumption of soft drinks and frequent junk food are major contributors to uncontrolled blood sugar.
priority_changes: Eliminate all sugary beverages including soft drinks.
Increase daily intake of vegetables and fruits.
Practice strict portion control for all meals.
diet_advice.breakfast: 2 Whole Wheat Idlis with Sambar and 1/2 cup Chana Dal Chutney. — Provides complex carbs, protein, and fiber for stable blood sugar.
diet_advice.lunch: 2 Multigrain Rotis, 1 cup Dal, 1.5 cups Mixed Vegetable Curry, Small Cucumber Salad. — Offers balanced macronutrients, high fiber, and essential micronutrients.
diet_advice.dinner: 1 cup Vegetable Daliya with Mixed Vegetables and 1/2 cup Paneer. — Light, easily digestible meal providing complex carbs, fiber, and protein.
foods_to_avoid: Soft drinks and packaged fruit juices — directly cause rapid blood sugar spikes.
Fried snacks and processed foods — high in unhealthy fats, sodium, and refined carbohydrates.
Refined sugar and sugary desserts — contribute to poor glycemic control.
diet_advice.clinical_note: Urgent medical review for significant HbA1c worsening to 9.2% and apparent discontinuation of antidiabetic medications.
